channel.title: Comments for page "Fire on the Horizon"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Fire on the Horizon"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• Love the entire opening sequence.
  • The honey conversation is good, but a bit too on the nose.
  • Ditto with the final conversation.
  • Fixed dialogue tag errors & a couple typos.

It's weird reading this after it's become a name people reference in association with Djoric and Dmatix a lot, but this is really goddamn intriguing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Obviously, it's because they're being stopped. It's hammer time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love this. Didn't notice the tale before.
Anyways, a question I've been pondering earlier, and asked Djoric; this makes it a little clearer.
Where does Project Malleus's name come from? Specifically, the first part. A project is generally something with a well defined goal. Is it "bring the hammer down on CotBG and Fifth Church" as the set goal of Project Malleus?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This I like. I wonder what their standpoint is vis-a-vis other religions. Still not sure how I feel about the establishment of a new religious GoI, but this is interesting. +1

A thought occurs: shouldn't they be aware of more anomalous cults than just the ones the Foundation knows about? It seems a bit odd that an anomalous interfaith organization would only be attempting to stop two other organizations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed. So awesome I might use it for a Horizon Initiative SCP I have in the works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
there's no hierarchy to heresy.

Awesome line, that is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So much good stuff packed into this. I love that they place the Fifthists on the same level as the CotBG. I suppose, from a spiritual standpoint, there's no hierarchy to heresy.

Anyway, this is a great addition and I hope you'll show us more later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite possibly. I think I'd like to show more of the Horizon Initiative's relationship with the Manna Charitable Foundation next.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is really cool. The three major Abrahamic religions join forces to kick Fifthist and CotBG ass? Fuck yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Iiiiinteresting. A secret order in the Catholic Church dedicated to finding and hiding "heretical" objects isn't that unique, but I like how you've included representatives of similar agencies within all three of major Abrahamic religions.

And you can't beat a good, old-fashioned Holy War.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, glad you were able to bring it out of sandboxing mode. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not really sure what to make of this… it seems like i'm reading the third chapter of a story I missed the beginning of, and since i've been playing through the Assassin's Creed series I have this unshakable urge to read the main character's lines as a deep-voiced middle-aged Italian.

+1 in the end, because it's well written, I want to know more, and it plugs the MCF.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely enjoyed this one, and as a bonus it leaves me wanting much more. +1.
